body.title: Librarian: become_knowledgeable_about persistent query
body.result: become_knowledgeable_about + add_hit + compress + get_inquiry in persistent_inquiry.py; adc.palace storage; 19 tests (12 unit + 7 integration against real Postgres)
body.intention: I intend that the Librarian can hold a standing inquiry that accumulates and synthesizes knowledge over time, distinct from one-shot research reports.
body.description:
learn(topic) produces a one-shot report and terminates. There is no mechanism for 'become knowledgeable about X' — a question that persists, recognizes relevant information as it arrives, and deepens its model over time rather than closing. This is the Radar O'Reilly pattern applied to the Librarian: not waiting to be asked, but maintaining a running model of things Akien/Igor care about and updating it when new information bears on them.

Proposed shape: become_knowledgeable_about(topic) creates a PersistentQuestion record in the palace. Each time relevant information flows through the Librarian (research calls, igor reads, etc.), the record accumulates hits weighted by novelty: confirmation (small) < gap-explanation (medium) < serendipitous discovery (strongest). Periodically or on request, accumulated hits compress into an updated current_model. Returns the current model on query. Distinct from the watchlist (monitoring for changes) — this is accumulation toward understanding.

**Affected files:** TBD — discovery step in sprint; likely a new persistent_inquiry.py in the Librarian device, palace schema additions for PersistentQuestion node type
**Design rules:** external state principle (state in palace/DB, not in-memory only); no-sqlite; Igor uses systems not contains them
**Scope boundary:** In scope: PersistentQuestion CRUD, hit accumulation with novelty weighting, compression step, query interface. Out of scope: automatic injection into all Librarian flows (wire-in separately), UI, changing existing learn() behavior.
**Completion criteria:** become_knowledgeable_about('X') creates a PersistentQuestion in the palace that survives restart. Manual hit injection appends to it. get_inquiry('X') returns the current_model including accumulated hits. At least one compression cycle runs and produces a shorter, denser model than the raw hit list. Tests cover: create, hit accumulation with differential weighting, compression, query, restart persistence.
